purpose: Human-made quality rubric for Alauda technical presales decks. Use this to judge generation quality across rotating page samples, not to hand-polish one SVG.
referenceSignals: Alauda content pages feel like engineered diagrams first, decorated slides second.
Dense pages preserve clear functional grouping through bands, rails, separators, and aligned labels.
The page title is quiet and precise; the main visual carries the message.
Technical labels are short, horizontal, and deliberately placed; no rotated CJK labels.
Side explanations are compact proof bullets, not generic copy blocks.
Brand color is used as structure and emphasis, not as full-page blue fill by default.

narrativeSpecificity.excellent: Each page has one technical sales job and the visual grammar matches that job.
narrativeSpecificity.poor: The page is a generic card grid that could fit any topic.

compositionHierarchy.excellent: Viewer can scan title, key message, primary diagram, and secondary proof in that order.
compositionHierarchy.poor: All elements compete equally, or the largest shape is not the most important idea.

densityControl.excellent: Dense architecture detail is grouped; whitespace and separators explain relationships.
densityControl.poor: Density is achieved by shrinking fonts, adding many boxes, or stacking labels.

brandNativeExecution.excellent: Uses Alauda frame, blue/gray structure, component library, and chunk icons consistently.
brandNativeExecution.poor: Looks like a generic AI template with random rounded cards or handmade icons.

technicalLegibility.excellent: Labels remain editable, horizontal, non-overlapping, and readable in PowerPoint.
technicalLegibility.poor: Labels collide with icons, arrows, separators, borders, or each other.

clientReadiness.excellent: A presales engineer could present it to a customer after content review.
clientReadiness.poor: Requires visual cleanup before an internal review.

pageArchetypes.platform_panorama: Human target: product-family map with domain bands, platform core, infra row, and sparse side value rail.
pageArchetypes.architecture_stack: Human target: layered operating architecture with clear control-plane/workload-plane relationships and compact proof bullets.
pageArchetypes.migration_bridge: Human target: current-state to target-state transition diagram with a visible bridge and risk-control path.
pageArchetypes.mapping_table: Human target: editable compact mapping table with short object names and visual status cues.
pageArchetypes.process_flow: Human target: four-step implementation path with numbered anchors and a single forward direction.
pageArchetypes.timeline: Human target: milestone rail that shows waves or phases without becoming a generic roadmap.
pageArchetypes.capability_canvas: Human target: three-domain operating canvas with capability clustering, not equal decorative cards.
pageArchetypes.comparison: Human target: before/after proof page with asymmetric emphasis on improvement.
pageArchetypes.evidence_argument: Human target: one thesis, three proof points, enough negative space for persuasion.
pageArchetypes.kpi_metrics: Human target: numbers as evidence, with restrained context and no decorative KPI stuffing.
pageArchetypes.risk_matrix: Human target: readable quadrant classification with risk-control interpretation.
pageArchetypes.code_sample: Human target: code/config snippet plus side annotations; avoid screenshot-like fake code.
pageArchetypes.screenshot_evidence: Human target: real product evidence framed with caption and callouts.
pageArchetypes.parallel_value: Human target: two parallel value streams with shared outcome, not two unrelated panels.

policy: Pick non-structural pages from different pageIntent values.
Always include at least one dense technical diagram page when the deck has one.
Prefer page intents not used in the previous quality iteration.
Do not use the same first sample page across consecutive iterations unless the source deck has fewer than four content pages.
When improving the skill itself, rotate sample page numbers and route intents instead of repeatedly editing the same generated SVG.

hardStops: Any visible text overlap, clipping, or out-of-canvas label.
Any off-contract icon library or handmade pseudo-icon when an approved icon exists.
Any dense page that uses generic symmetric cards instead of the routed visual grammar.
Any CJK label rotated vertically or placed in a narrow unreadable strip.
